title: CUBIC-HistoVIsion2.0
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"i4cgx","text":"This protocol describes a method for simple and scalable whole-organ immunostaining, termed CUBIC-HistoVIsion 2.0 (CUBIC-HV2.0). The commercialized product developed based on this protocol are available from CUBICStars, Inc. (https://www.cubicstars.com/en/lineup/).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":226,"length":37}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"C4BB98B6FEAA11F0BA210A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.cubicstars.com/en/lineup"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6mjic","text":"We strongly recommend that you first test this method with a benchmark experiment, specifically conducting anti-NeuN immunostaining with SYTOX-G nuclear staining on an entire mouse brain.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Anesthetize the mouse.
Immerse the delipidated sample in 10 mL of PBS containing 5% MeOH in the 15 mL standing tube and incubate it for Overnight at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).
Immerse the fixed sample in 10 mL of 0.5× CUBIC-L (1:1 dilution with water) in the 30 mL tube and incubate it Overnight at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).

During the sample wash, prepare up to 10 mL of fixative solution by diluting formalin (FA) solution to 1% in HV wash buffer and cool it On ice . The saturated formalin solution contains 35 to 38% of formalin. For example, when using a 37% formalin solution, dilute it with HV wash buffer at a ratio of 1:36.
Transcardially perfuse 10 mL (4 mL/min) of ice-cold PBS containing 10 U/mL of Heparin.
Transcardially perfuse 20 mL (6 mL/min) of ice-cold 4% (w/v) PFA.
Dissect the organs.
Wash the sample in PBS containing 0.05% NaN 3 for 01:00:00 × 3 times at Room temperature with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).
Post-fix the dissected organs in 4% (w/v) PFA in PBS (~ 10 mL /whole organ) for Overnight at 4 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).

Wash the sample with 20 mL of PBS containing 0.05% NaN 3 for 02:00:00 × 3 times (or 02:00:00 ×1, Overnight ×1, 02:00:00 ×1) at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ). After each use, the tubes should be washed or replaced in order to remove Triton X-100 thoroughly. The delipidated sample can be stored in PBS containing 0.05% NaN 3 at 4 °C for at least several months.

Replace with 10 mL to 15 mL of 1× CUBIC-L in the 30 mL tube and delipidate for 72:00:00 to 120:00:00 (3 to 5 days) at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ). Replace CUBIC-L every 48:00:00 to 72:00:00 (2 to 3 days) if the duration of treatment exceeds 72:00:00 (3 days).
Replace the HV wash buffer with the new one and continue washing the sample two more times ( 02:00:00 ×2).
Replace the HV wash buffer with a new one and continue washing the sample gently at 4 °C with light shielding, while shaking at 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, , for 02:00:00 .
Immerse the sample in 10 mL of pre-cooled HV wash buffer in the 15 mL standing tube. Gently invert the tube several times to separate the wrapping reagent remaining on the sample surface.
Remove the residual buffer and the HV-LLPS reagent as in step 14.
Recover the sample from the staining pot with a metal spoon. Discard the staining solution and the HV-LLPS reagent.
To stabilize the 2nd Fab signals, further incubate the staining chamber for 24:00:00 at 4 °C with slow rotation (< 1 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.
Close the lid tightly and incubate the chamber for at least 168:00:00 (1 week) at Room temperature with slow rotation (< 1 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

Slowly pour the HV-LLPS reagent into the staining pot until the pot is filled. Remove the bubbles. HV-LLPS prevents samples from drying out during staining by covering the entire sample and the staining solution, which contains condensed staining probes.

Exchange the buffer to 10 mL of HV staining buffer (10 mM HEPES [pH 7.5], 10% [w/v] Triton X-100, 500 mM NaCl, and 0.5% [w/v] casein) containing HV-Additive suitable for your primary Ab and incubate it for 01:30:00 at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).
Collect the sample with a metal spoon and put it into the staining pot. A portion of the sample should be attached gently on soft paper towel to absorb residual buffer.

Transfer the entire 3D staining solution into the 3D tissue staining pot ( CUBICStars, CSSR005 ). This 3D tissue staining pot for CUBIC-HV2.0 can be used repeatedly. After the usage, wash the pot with neutral detergent and distilled water.

During the step 11, prepare primary Ab + secondary Fab fragment mix as follows: Calculate the required volume (X, Y) of primary Ab and secondary Fab fragment. Example of calculation is shown bel %s %s ow. %s %s Prepare the following 3D staining solution (typically 50 to 100 µL per whole mouse brain). Example of preparation is shown below. A B 1 Reagent Volume 2 2× HV staining buffer containing HV-Additive 50 µL 3 Primary Ab X µL 4 Secondary Fab Y µL 5 Nuclear stain* Z µL 6 Distilled water 50-(X+Y+Z) µL 7 Total 100 µL The total volume of the staining solution should be determined based on the quantity and final concentration of primary Ab. For instance, in the case of using 5 µg of primary Ab at a concentration of 100 µg/mL, a volume of 50 µL should be prepared for the 3D staining solution (5 µg of primary Ab in 50 µL = 100 µg/mL). Generally, 50-100 µg/mL of antibody concentration prepared in 50-350 µL of the staining solution is recommended. The type of HV-additives should be determined for each primary antibody (please check "Before start" section). Generally, one or two HV-Additive reagent are used. *Recommended amount of nuclear stain BOBO-1 Iodide: 4 µL/whole mouse brain SYTOX Green Nucleic Acid Stain: 1.6 µL/whole mouse brain RedDot2 Far-Red Nuclear Stain: 4 µL/whole mouse brain Add the entire volume to the 500 µL Protein LoBind tube and incubate the mix for 01:30:00 at 37 °C .

Incubate the sample in 15 mL of 0.5× CUBIC-R+ (1:1 diluted with distilled water) in a 30 mL tube for 24:00:00 at > 25 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

Remove the residual diluted CUBIC-R+ as in section 3-step 5 and incubate the sample in 15 mL (or 30 mL if the tissue will be embedded in agarose gel) of 1× CUBIC-R+ for 48:00:00 (2 days) at > 25 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition. Carryover of residual diluted CUBIC-R+ results in a poor clearing result due to a decrease in the final refractive index. CUBIC-R+ crystallizes easily in winter. Ensure that sample in the reagent are handled and incubated at 25°C or higher. If required, CUBIC-R+-cleared samples can be embedded in 2% agarose/CUBIC-R+. For more details, please refer to our protocol paper and our resource website.
Use the sample for microscopic observation (gel embedding if necessary)
Wash the sample in 30 mL of PBS in the 50 mL tube for 02:00:00 at Room temperature with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

To accelerate the fixation reaction, further incubate the sample for 01:00:00 at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition. Reducing the FA reaction time in steps 25 and 26 could result in the attenuation of antibody signals.
Fix the sample in the 1% FA for 24:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.
One hour before moving to the step 19, prepare HV wash buffer (10 mM HEPES [pH 7.5], 10% [w/v] Triton X-100, 500 mM NaCl) and cool it On ice .
creator: Reo Kurusu
